page: Google Cloud Platform c3d-highmem-90 is a memory-optimized server instance powered by an AMD EPYC 9B14 processor utilizing the x86_64 architecture. It features 90 dedicated vCPUs mapped to 45 physical cores with simultaneous multithreading at 2.0 threads per core. The system is equipped with 720 GB of RAM, providing a high memory-to-vCPU ratio of 8.0 GB per core. It does not include local storage or bundled GPUs, and has zero complimentary public IPv4 addresses.

Benchmark results indicate top-tier multi-core CPU performance, with stress-ng multi-core, PassMark CPU, and PassMark CPU extended instructions tests all scoring in the top 10%. The processor supports advanced instruction sets including AVX-512 and cryptography acceleration. Memory bandwidth is strong, placing in the top 25% for read-only operations, though large-block write bandwidth drops to average. Redis SET operations and software compilation with Clang also achieve top-tier performance.

Given its high memory density and strong multi-core compute capabilities, this instance is suited for memory-intensive workloads such as large-scale database operations, software compilation, and medium-scale LLM inference prompt processing. While it lacks local storage and GPU acceleration, its high resource density offers qualitative cost efficiency for workloads requiring substantial RAM without the overhead of dedicated graphics hardware.
description: Google Cloud Platform c3d-highmem-90 is a memory-optimized server configured with 90 dedicated vCPUs and 720 GB of RAM. Powered by an AMD EPYC 9B14 processor on the x86_64 architecture, it features 45 physical cores with simultaneous multithreading. The instance lacks local storage and GPU accelerators. Benchmark data highlights top-tier multi-core CPU performance, with PassMark CPU and stress-ng multi-core scores in the top 10%. It also demonstrates strong memory read bandwidth and top-tier Redis SET throughput. The server is well-suited for memory-intensive workloads, database operations, software compilation, and medium-scale LLM inference tasks.
